skill.name: maxhub-bilibili
skill.description: B站视频、用户、评论、弹幕、直播数据查询助手。支持App和Web双端API。
skill.summary: Query Bilibili video, user, comment, danmaku, and live stream data through the MaxHub API.
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 460 static findings: all are false positives from scanning Markdown documentation as source code. Ruby backtick execution detections are Markdown code fence syntax showing curl examples. Weak cryptographic algorithm detections are documentation text, not actual crypto code. System reconnaissance flags are example parameter values in API documentation tables. The skill is a legitimate read-only Bilibili data query tool that makes API calls to a predefined endpoint using a user-configured API key.

low_findings.title: Markdown code fences misinterpreted as shell execution
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected backtick syntax in Markdown files as Ruby shell execution. All findings are Markdown code fences (```bash) showing curl command examples for the API. No actual command execution occurs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All reference files are Markdown documentation with no executable scripts. Backtick patterns are exclusively Markdown code block syntax.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs are legitimate API endpoints
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged hardcoded URLs across multiple files. All URLs point to https://www.aconfig.cn, which is the legitimate API endpoint for the MaxHub service. These are not malicious network destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every URL points to the same known API service. The SKILL.md explicitly documents this as the API base URL with full transparency.

low_findings.title: API key environment variable reference is configuration guidance
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged MAXHUB_API_KEY references as exposed secrets. These are documentation showing users how to configure the required environment variable. No actual API keys are hardcoded in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All env_access references use shell variable syntax ($MAXHUB_API_KEY) in documentation examples, never hardcoded credential values.

low_findings.title: API documentation text misidentified as weak cryptography
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged text in reference API documentation as weak cryptographic algorithms. The reference files contain parameter descriptions and response format documentation, not cryptographic code. No actual encryption or hashing is performed by this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All reference files are pure Markdown documentation with no code blocks or cryptographic implementations.

low_findings.title: API parameter examples misidentified as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged example parameter values (user IDs, video IDs, URLs) as system reconnaissance. These are documentation examples in API parameter tables showing users what values to provide. No actual data collection or scanning occurs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged locations are within Markdown table rows showing parameter names, types, and example values for API documentation.

low_findings.title: Dangerous combination heuristic is false positive for documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged the combination of network access, credential access, and shell command patterns. All three factors are from Markdown documentation files, not executable code. The skill is a read-only API client with clearly documented intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: External commands are documented curl examples in code blocks. Network targets are the documented API endpoint. Credential access is user-provided API key configuration.

content.user_title: Query Bilibili video, user, and live data with MaxHub API
content.value_statement: Accessing Bilibili data programmatically requires complex API reverse engineering and authentication handling. This skill provides 41 curated API endpoints through the MaxHub service for querying videos, user profiles, comments, danmaku, and live streams with a single API key.
actual_capabilities: Query Bilibili video details including title, description, play count, and upload time
Retrieve user profile information, follower counts, and video upload history
Search for videos, users, and content across Bilibili categories
Fetch video comments, replies, and danmaku (real-time subtitles)
Get live stream room details and currently streaming creators
Access hot search trends, popular feeds, and category recommendations
limitations: Requires a MaxHub API key from aconfig.cn for all requests
Some endpoints may return 403 if the API lacks platform session cookies
Data is provided by a third-party API and may have caching delays
Cannot perform write operations such as posting comments or uploading videos

use_cases.title: UP creator evaluation for brand partnerships
use_cases.description: Marketing professionals can search for Bilibili creators, analyze their follower metrics, view their video history, and evaluate engagement rates to find suitable partners.

use_cases.title: Video content trend analysis
use_cases.description: Content teams can fetch popular video feeds, analyze video details, and examine comment sentiment to understand what content performs well on Bilibili.

use_cases.title: Community topic research
use_cases.description: Community运营 teams can explore hot search trends, category recommendations, and live stream data to identify emerging topics and user interests.

prompt_templates.title: Single video lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the details for Bilibili video BV1M1421t7hT
prompt_templates.scenario: Querying basic information for one video

prompt_templates.title: User profile and content analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze Bilibili UP主 with uid 178360345. Get their profile, recent videos, and follower statistics.

prompt_templates.title: Trending content search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for popular Bilibili videos about artificial intelligence. Get top results with view counts and upload dates.

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive channel deep dive
prompt_templates.prompt: Do a full analysis of Bilibili user uid 178360345. First get their profile information, then fetch their uploaded videos, and finally get the top comments on their most viewed video.
prompt_templates.scenario: Multi-endpoint orchestration for in-depth channel analysis

output_examples.input: Get the details for Bilibili video BV1M1421t7hT
output_examples.output:
Here are the details for this video:

**Title:** [Video title appears here]
**Uploader:** [Creator name]
**Views:** 1.2M
**Likes:** 45.6K
**Comments:** 2.3K
**Upload date:** 2025-03-15

**Description:** [Video description text appears here]

output_examples.input: Search for popular Bilibili videos about cooking
output_examples.output:
Found 15 results for cooking videos:

| Title | Uploader | Views | Duration |
|-------|----------|-------|----------|
| [Title 1] | [Creator] | 890K | 12:30 |
| [Title 2] | [Creator] | 654K | 8:15 |
| [Title 3] | [Creator] | 432K | 15:45 |

**Key findings:** The top 3 videos are recipe tutorials with high engagement.

best_practices: Configure the MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable before making any queries to ensure authentication works on the first request.
Use specific search terms and user IDs instead of broad queries to get more relevant results and reduce API call volume.
Fetch video details and viewer statistics together in one session to create complete content analysis reports.
anti_patterns: Do NOT hardcode the API key in prompts or skill files. Always use the MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable for secure credential management.
Do NOT attempt to modify Bilibili data through this skill. It is a read-only query tool and cannot post comments, upload videos, or change settings.
Do NOT share the API key or session cookies with third parties. Treat the MaxHub API key as sensitive credential data.

faq.question: How do I get an API key for this skill?
faq.answer: Sign up at https://www.aconfig.cn and create an API key in your dashboard. Then set it as the MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Chinese and English?
faq.answer: The skill supports both Chinese and English. It detects your language and responds in the same language with appropriate number formatting.

faq.question: Can I post comments or upload videos with this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill is read-only and designed only for data querying. It cannot perform any write operations on Bilibili.

faq.question: What data about a user can I get?
faq.answer: You can get user profile information, follower counts, following counts, video upload history, collection folders, and dynamic feeds.

faq.question: How many API calls can I make?
faq.answer: The API is subject to rate limiting by the MaxHub service. If you receive a 429 error, wait before making additional requests.

faq.question: Where does the data come from?
faq.answer: Data is provided by the MaxHub API service at www.aconfig.cn. It aggregates data from Bilibili public APIs and is provided as-is for reference.
